extracted.headquarters: 3200 Windy Hill Road SE, Suite 1200E, Atlanta, GA 30339
extracted.brandBackground: Primrose Schools offers high-quality educational childcare services for children from six weeks to twelve years old using a proprietary Balanced Learning curriculum.

leadership.name: David Berg
leadership.background: Served as CEO of EWC Franchisor LLC (European Wax Center) and on its Board of Directors.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings extensive executive leadership experience in high-growth franchise systems.

leadership.name: Rob Gray
leadership.background: Held multiple financial roles at PSFC including Chief Accounting Officer and VP of Finance.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides deep continuity and oversight of the brand's financial operations.

leadership.name: Kristin Goran
leadership.background: Served as Chief Legal Officer of PSFC and General Counsel of PHC.
leadership.whyItMatters: Ensures legal compliance and robust franchise system governance.

leadership.name: Amy Jackson
leadership.background: Served as Chief Early Learning Strategy Officer of PSFC and Adjunct Professor at Johns Hopkins University.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings academic and strategic early childhood education expertise to franchise operations.

leadership.name: Greg Foglesong
leadership.background: Served as Chief Commercial Officer of Monica + Andy, Inc. and Chief Marketing and Digital Officer of Bob's Discount Furniture.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings strong retail marketing and commercial strategy experience to drive enrollment.

leadership.name: Pam Turner
leadership.background: Served as Chief of Staff of PSFC and VP of Operations Strategy for Hand and Stone Franchise LLC.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees technology systems and digital infrastructure critical for school operations.

item19.notes: EBITDAR excludes rent, interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. EBITDA includes rent expenses.

lineItems.notes: Standard initial franchise fee for a new franchisee.
Fee for real estate and development services.
Includes site development, construction, and professional fees.

lineItems.category: Security Deposit and Rent Guarantee Fee
lineItems.notes: Security deposit for lease and potential rent guarantee fee.
Deposits required by utility companies before opening.
Furniture, fixtures, equipment, and teaching materials.
Estimated cost for the first quarter of insurance premiums.
Covers initial training and opening support services.
Lease payments for a required student transportation vehicle.
Business licenses and permits required to operate.
Storage, legal fees, and pre-opening payroll.
Finance charges and loan fees prior to closing.
Working capital for the first three months of operation.

hiddenCosts.name: Construction Management Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Fee charged if the franchisor or its affiliate is designated as the construction manager.

hiddenCosts.name: National Architect Prototype Release Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Fee for releasing prototype plans if using an outside architect.

hiddenCosts.name: National Architect Plan Review Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Fee to review plans designed by an outside architect.

hiddenCosts.name: Rent Guarantee Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Fee equal to 5% of the rent guarantee amount if provided by PSFC.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by arbitration or litigation in Georgia, which may increase costs.

operationalRisks.title: Unopened Franchises
operationalRisks.description: A significant number of signed franchise agreements have not yet opened, indicating potential development delays.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees' spouses must sign personal guarantees, placing personal and marital assets at risk.

extracted.conceptRationale: Primrose Schools is a leading educational childcare franchise system offering early education and care services.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires on-premises supervision by an On-Site Owner, a Director, and certified teachers.

riskReasons: Capital gap of $6,238,450 is ~25x your liquid — extreme leverage; the deal rests almost entirely on outside capital.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.

midCohort.label: Second Quartile Occupancy Rate Schools Gross Revenue

source.label: Second Quartile Occupancy Rate Schools Gross Revenue

bottomCohort.label: Bottom Quartile Occupancy Rate Schools Gross Revenue

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $6,488,450 mid-point build-out, there is a $6,238,450 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $119,991/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: Primrose Schools is a leading educational childcare franchise system offering early education and care services.

benchmark.label: Education / childcare
benchmark.dominantRisk: Regulated staff ratios and enrollment/utilization. Labor dominates and ratios cap how lean you can run.
considerations: Labor is the dominant line at ~40–55% and is floored by regulated student/staff ratios.
Validate enrollment ramp and capacity-utilization curves with Item 20 operators.
Confirm licensing, facility, and compliance costs specific to your state — not in the FDD.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 29.9–41.1 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Education / childcare” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken from the FDD (~$28,192/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 5–12% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 40–55% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. Primrose Holding Corporation guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
